Name: UPhysicsSpringComponent
Description:
Note: this component is still work in progress. Uses raycast springs for simple vehicle forces
 	Used with objects that have physics to create a spring down the X direction
 	ie. point X in the direction you want generate spring.

Variables.Name: SpringStiffness
Variables.Description: Specifies how much strength the spring has. The higher the SpringStiffness the more force the spring can push on a body with.

Variables.Name: SpringDamping
Variables.Description: Specifies how quickly the spring can absorb energy of a body. The higher the damping the less oscillation

Variables.Name: SpringLengthAtRest
Variables.Description: Determines how long the spring will be along the X-axis at rest. The spring will apply 0 force on a body when it's at rest.

Variables.Name: SpringRadius
Variables.Description: Determines the radius of the spring.

Variables.Name: SpringChannel
Variables.Description: Strength of thrust force applied to the base object.

Variables.Name: bIgnoreSelf
Variables.Description: If true, the spring will ignore all components in its own actor

Variables.Name: SpringCompression
Variables.Description: The current compression of the spring. A spring at rest will have SpringCompression 0.

Functions.Name: GetNormalizedCompressionScalar
Functions.Description:
Returns the spring compression as a normalized scalar along spring direction.
	   0 implies spring is at rest
	   1 implies fully compressed

Functions.Name: GetSpringRestingPoint
Functions.Description: Returns the spring resting point in world space.

Functions.Name: GetSpringCurrentEndPoint
Functions.Description: Returns the spring current end point in world space.

Functions.Name: GetSpringDirection
Functions.Description: Returns the spring direction from start to resting point
